description: In Hell, Charlie Morningstar, the princess of Hell, pursues her seemingly impossible goal of rehabilitating demons to peacefully reduce overpopulation in her kingdom. She opens a hotel in hopes that patients will "check out" into Heaven.

episodes.title: That's Entertainment (Pilot)
episodes.desc: Charlie Morningstar introduces her ambitious project to the public through a chaotic live television broadcast, hoping to save sinner souls from the yearly purge.

episodes.title: Addict (Music Video Special)
episodes.desc: A deeper look into Angel Dust and Cherri Bomb's turbulent lives, coping mechanisms, and struggles under the oppressive rule of Valentino in the Pride Ring.

episodes.title: Look My Way (Music Video Feature)
episodes.desc: An emotional musical showcase exploring the complicated web of devotion, past mistakes, and long-distance ties impacting key residents of the hotel network.

episodes.title: Overture
episodes.desc: Charlie pitches her project to heaven, running into aggressive bureaucratic red tape while trying to prove that sinners deserve a chance at redemption.

episodes.title: Radio Killed the Video Star
episodes.desc: Alastor protects the hotel from Vox and the modern media empire of the Vees as old rivalries flare up on Hell's broadcast networks.

episodes.title: Scrambled Eggs
episodes.desc: Trust exercises cause chaos among the crew while Sir Pentious tries to infiltrate the hotel with an army of loyal but clumsy Egg Boiz.

episodes.title: Masquerade
episodes.desc: Angel Dust struggles with his toxic double life as an adult media star under Valentino's demanding contract, forcing Husk to step in with hard truths.

episodes.title: Dad Beat Dad
episodes.desc: Charlie struggles to connect with her eccentric father, King Lucifer, while attempting to secure an urgent meeting with Heaven's higher management.

episodes.title: Welcome to Heaven
episodes.desc: Charlie and Vaggie travel directly to the pearly gates of Heaven to present their case, causing a massive stir among the high-ranking Seraphim.

episodes.title: Hello Rosie
episodes.desc: With the fast-approaching extermination deadline, Charlie travels to Cannibal Town to bargain for an army with the influential Overlord Rosie.

episodes.title: The Show Must Go On
episodes.desc: An all-out war erupts at the hotel gates as Adam and his angelic exorcist legion clash with the hotel residents in a final, brutal showdown.

episodes.title: New Pentious
episodes.desc: Sir Pentious adjusts to his unexpected new reality in Heaven, navigating celestial social hierarchies while the hotel updates its mission statement down below.

episodes.title: Storyteller
episodes.desc: Secrets of Lilith's mysterious absence and past bargains begin to unravel as new dynamic threats eye up the power vacuum left in the kingdom.

episodes.title: Hazbin Hotel: Behind Closed Doors
episodes.desc: The hotel faces new structural challenges and internal disputes as daily therapeutic counseling tests the limits of the demonic residents.

episodes.title: It's A Deal
episodes.desc: Alastor's tight-lipped bargains and underlying motivations come back into heavy play, shifting alliances among the overlords of Hell.

episodes.title: Silenced
episodes.desc: An unexpected radio silence falls over Hell's media grid, triggering widespread paranoia regarding an impending systematic purge or regional revolt.

episodes.title: Scream Rain
episodes.desc: Tensions boil over into an all-out storm inside the Pride Ring as internal team disagreements reach an explosive, emotional breaking point.

episodes.title: Weapon of Mass Distraction
episodes.desc: The team scrambles to build a defensive perimeter and secure strategic fallback measures ahead of an aggressive counter-offensive by opposing factions.

episodes.title: Curtain Call
episodes.desc: The thrilling conclusion to the season's intense arc, bringing massive cosmic consequences to both Heaven and Hell as the hotel takes a definitive stand.
